info.title: Beans News API & MCP
info.description:
MCP-ready news and blog intelligence over RSS-sourced articles, semantic enrichment, and propagation tracking.
A **bean** is one article or post keyed by canonical URL. Records include publisher metadata, summary/full text, publish timestamp, inferred categories, regions, entities, sentiments, and optional social trend metrics.
Agent workflow: (1) listCategories, listEntities, listRegions to discover exact filter values; (2) searchArticles for full-corpus retrieval; (3) getLatestArticles, getTrendingArticles, or getTopHeadlines for feed-style monitoring; (4) getPublishers to resolve source IDs; (5) getArticlePropagation or postArticlePropagation to check story spread.
Conventions: Auth is optional at the backend but API-key protected through the gateway. Pagination uses `limit` default 16 max 128 and `offset` default 0. Empty result sets return HTTP 204, not an error. Use fuzzy `tags` when spelling is uncertain, exact `categories`/`regions`/`entities` after discovery, and `q` + `acc` for semantic vector search.

tags.name: Articles
tags.description: Trending, latest, and searchable news and blog articles

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Unique categories, entities, and regions for filtering articles

Bean.title: Bean
Bean.description: Primary article/post object returned by Beans article endpoints. Agents should treat `url` as the stable identifier, `source` as the publisher id, `summary` as the compact context field, and `content` as optional full text only present when requested. `categories`, `regions`, `entities`, `sentiments`, and `tags` are inferred enrichment fields for filtering and grounding responses. Internal embedding and gist fields are used for search but omitted from JSON.

url.description: URL is the canonical URL of the article or post.

content_type.description: Kind is the content type, for example news, blog, post, generated, or comment.

title.description: Title is the human-readable headline or title of the content.

summary.description: Summary is a short abstract or teaser used in listings and previews.

content.description: Content is the full body text when the source content is available.

author.description: Author is the byline or attributed creator when available from the source.

source.description: Source is the canonical publisher identifier and matches Publisher.Source.

image_url.description: ImageUrl is the featured image or preview image associated with the content.

published_at.description: Created is the original publish timestamp of the article or post.

categories.description: Categories lists the inferred topics assigned to the content.

regions.description: Regions lists geographic regions mentioned in or associated with the content.

entities.description: Entities lists named entities such as people, places, organizations, or products.

tags.description: Computed tags merged from categories/regions/entities for display

sentiments.description: Sentiments lists inferred tones or sentiments expressed in the content.

BeanTrend.description: Article/post object plus trend analytics. Returned by trending and top-headline endpoints when an agent needs both article context and engagement ranking fields such as likes, comments, shares, related count, and trend_score.

url.description: URL is the canonical URL of the article or post.

title.description: Title is the human-readable headline or title of the content.

summary.description: Summary is a short abstract or teaser used in listings and previews.

content.description: Content is the full body text when the source content is available.

content_type.description: Kind is the content type, for example news, blog, post, generated, or comment.

author.description: Author is the byline or attributed creator when available from the source.

image_url.description: ImageUrl is the featured image or preview image associated with the content.

source.description: Source is the canonical publisher identifier and matches Publisher.Source.

categories.description: Categories lists the inferred topics assigned to the content.

regions.description: Regions lists geographic regions mentioned in or associated with the content.

entities.description: Entities lists named entities such as people, places, organizations, or products.

tags.description: Computed tags merged from categories/regions/entities for display

published_at.description: Created is the original publish timestamp of the article or post.

likes.description: Likes is the aggregate number of likes or upvotes associated with this Bean.

comments.description: Comments is the aggregate number of replies or comments associated with this Bean.

shares.description: Shares is the aggregate number of reposts or share-like actions associated with this Bean.

subscribers.description: Subscribers is the aggregate audience size associated with this Bean's chatter.

related.description: Related is the count of semantically or editorially related Beans.

trend_score.description: TrendScore is the computed ranking score used to order trending results.

sentiments.description: Sentiments lists inferred tones or sentiments expressed in the content.

BeanAggregate.description: Search result object that combines article content, social/trend metrics, and publisher display fields. Returned by searchArticles so agents can cite the article and identify the source without making a separate publisher lookup.

url.description: URL is the canonical URL of the article or post.

title.description: Title is the human-readable headline or title of the content.

summary.description: Summary is a short abstract or teaser used in listings and previews.

content.description: Content is the full body text when the source content is available.

content_type.description: Kind is the content type, for example news, blog, post, generated, or comment.

author.description: Author is the byline or attributed creator when available from the source.

image_url.description: ImageUrl is the featured image or preview image associated with the content.

source.description: Source is the canonical publisher identifier and matches Publisher.Source.

source_base_url.description: BaseURL is the publisher's primary site URL copied onto aggregate results for convenience.

source_description.description: Description is the publisher description copied onto aggregate results.

source_favicon.description: Favicon is the publisher favicon URL copied onto aggregate results.

source_site_name.description: SiteName is the human-readable name of the publisher copied onto aggregate results.

categories.description: Categories lists the inferred topics assigned to the content.

regions.description: Regions lists geographic regions mentioned in or associated with the content.

entities.description: Entities lists named entities such as people, places, organizations, or products.

tags.description: Computed tags merged from categories/regions/entities for display

published_at.description: Created is the original publish timestamp of the article or post.

likes.description: Likes is the aggregate number of likes or upvotes associated with this Bean.

comments.description: Comments is the aggregate number of replies or comments associated with this Bean.

shares.description: Shares is the aggregate number of reposts or share-like actions associated with this Bean.

subscribers.description: Subscribers is the aggregate audience size associated with this Bean's chatter.

related.description: Related is the count of semantically or editorially related Beans.

trend_score.description: TrendScore is the computed ranking score used to order trending results.

sentiments.description: Sentiments lists inferred tones or sentiments expressed in the content.

PropagationCoverage.description: One cross-publisher coverage hit for a seed article URL. Use it to see whether a story was republished or covered by another source.

PropagationMention.description: One social or forum mention for a seed article URL, including where it appeared and any available engagement counts.

PropagationResult.description: Propagation result for one input article URL. `coverage` shows related publisher articles; `mentions` shows social/forum discussion. Empty arrays mean no propagation was found for that URL.

urls.description: URLs lists seed article URLs to analyze for cross-outlet coverage and social mentions (1–128 items).

source.title: Source
source.description: Source is the canonical publisher identifier and matches Bean.Source values.

source_base_url.title: Source Base Url
source_base_url.description: BaseURL is the publisher's primary site URL.

source_description.title: Source Description
source_description.description: Description is a short description of the publisher or content source.

source_favicon.title: Source Favicon
source_favicon.description: Favicon is the URL of the publisher favicon or brand icon.

source_site_name.title: Source Site Name
source_site_name.description: SiteName is the human-readable display name of the publisher.

Publisher.title: Publisher
Publisher.description: Publisher/source metadata used to turn article `source` ids into human-readable site details. Use getPublishers when an agent needs display names, base URLs, descriptions, or favicons for sources returned by article endpoints.

get.description: Lightweight liveness probe. Use before other tools to confirm the service is reachable. No authentication required when API keys are disabled.

Discover valid values for the `categories` filter on article endpoints.
Returns a paginated array of unique topic labels extracted from indexed articles (e.g. "Artificial Intelligence", "Cybersecurity", "Politics").
**When to use**: call before searchArticles or feed endpoints when you need exact, case-sensitive category strings.
**Related tools**: listEntities, listRegions, searchArticles.
**Pagination**: use `offset` to walk the full catalog when `limit` < total count.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Default 0.

200.description: JSON array of category label strings

204.description: No categories in index (empty result, not an error)

get.description:
Discover valid values for the `entities` filter on article endpoints.
Returns a paginated array of unique named entities (people, organizations, products, places) extracted via NLP from article text.
**When to use**: call before searchArticles when filtering by specific people, companies, or places with exact spelling.
**Related tools**: listCategories, listRegions, searchArticles.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Default 0.

200.description: JSON array of entity label strings

204.description: No entities in index (empty result, not an error)

get.description:
Discover valid values for the `regions` filter on article endpoints.
Returns a paginated array of unique geographic region labels (e.g. "North America", "Europe", "India", "Middle East").
**When to use**: call before searchArticles when filtering by geography with exact, case-sensitive region strings.
**Related tools**: listCategories, listEntities, searchArticles.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Default 0.

200.description: JSON array of region label strings

204.description: No regions in index (empty result, not an error)

get.summary: Search or list top headlines from the last 24 hours
get.description:
Returns the highest trend_score articles from the past 24 hours — a narrow window on getTrendingArticles.
**When to use**: breaking news, daily briefings, or "what is hot today" without a custom date range.
**Note**: `from` is not accepted; the 24h window is fixed server-side.
**Filters** (all optional): same semantics as getTrendingArticles except no date override.
**Related tools**: getTrendingArticles (7-day window), searchArticles.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional semantic search query (3–512 chars).

parameters.name: acc
parameters.description: Match strictness when q is set. Default 0.5.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: Restrict to content kind: news or blog.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Fuzzy filter across categories+regions+entities (AND between values).

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Exact topic filter (OR).

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Exact region filter (OR).

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Exact entity filter (OR).

parameters.name: sources
parameters.description: Publisher source ID filter (OR).

parameters.name: full_content
parameters.description: Include full article body. Default false.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Skip N results. Default 0.

200.description: Top headlines from last 24h sorted by trend_score descending

204.description: No headlines in last 24h (empty result, not an error)

get.summary: Search or list newest articles (reverse chronological)
get.description:
Returns recently published articles sorted by publish date (newest first).
**Time window**: if `from` is omitted, defaults to the last 7 days.
**Filters** (all optional): same semantics as searchArticles — `q` for semantic search, `tags` for fuzzy match, or exact `categories`/`regions`/`entities`/`sources`.
**When to use**: monitoring recent news in a topic without full-corpus search cost. Lighter than searchArticles.
**Related tools**: listCategories, listEntities, listRegions, searchArticles, getTrendingArticles.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional semantic search query (3–512 chars). Narrows results by embedding similarity.

parameters.name: acc
parameters.description: Match strictness when q is set. Default 0.5.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: Restrict to content kind: news or blog.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Fuzzy filter across categories+regions+entities (AND between values).

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Exact topic filter (OR). Use listCategories for valid values.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Exact region filter (OR). Use listRegions for valid values.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Exact entity filter (OR). Use listEntities for valid values.

parameters.name: sources
parameters.description: Publisher source ID filter (OR).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Published on/after this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 7 days ago when omitted.

parameters.name: full_content
parameters.description: Include full article body. Default false.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Skip N results. Default 0.

200.description: Latest articles sorted by published_at descending

204.description: No articles in window (empty result, not an error)

get.summary: Search or list trending articles by engagement score
get.description:
Returns articles ranked by `trend_score` (highest first). Trend score blends social engagement (likes, comments, shares), cross-outlet coverage, and recency.
**Time window**: if `from` is omitted, defaults to the last 7 days of trending activity.
**Filters** (all optional): same semantics as searchArticles.
**When to use**: surface what is gaining traction now — prefer over getLatestArticles when popularity matters more than recency alone.
**Related tools**: getTopHeadlines (24h subset), searchArticles, getArticlePropagation.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional semantic search query (3–512 chars).

parameters.name: acc
parameters.description: Match strictness when q is set. Default 0.5.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: Restrict to content kind: news or blog.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Fuzzy filter across categories+regions+entities (AND between values).

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Exact topic filter (OR).

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Exact region filter (OR).

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Exact entity filter (OR).

parameters.name: sources
parameters.description: Publisher source ID filter (OR).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Trending activity since this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 7 days ago when omitted.

parameters.name: full_content
parameters.description: Include full article body. Default false.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Skip N results. Default 0.

200.description: Articles with engagement metrics and trend_score, sorted descending

204.description: No trending articles in window (empty result, not an error)

get.summary: Search all articles by topic, tags, or URL
get.description:
**Primary MCP tool** — full-corpus search sorted by relevance.
**Requires at least one of**: `q`, `tags`, `categories`, `regions`, `entities`, or `urls`.
**Search modes** (combinable with filters):
- `q` + `acc`: semantic vector search over article embeddings (natural language, 3–512 chars).
- `tags`: fuzzy text match across categories, regions, and entities (AND between tag values; case/whitespace insensitive).
- `categories` / `regions` / `entities`: exact array filters (OR within each dimension; case/whitespace sensitive — discover values via listCategories, listEntities, listRegions).
- `urls`: fetch specific articles by canonical URL (CSV).
**Performance**: semantic search uses indexed nearest-neighbor candidates; prefer `full_content=false` unless the body is needed. Heavier than feed endpoints.
**Related tools**: listCategories, listEntities, listRegions, getPublishers, getArticlePropagation.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Semantic search query in natural language (3–512 chars). Ranks by embedding similarity.

parameters.name: acc
parameters.description: Match strictness when q is set. 0.0=broad, 1.0=strict. Default 0.5.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.description: Restrict to content kind: news or blog.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description: Fetch articles by exact URL (CSV). Satisfies the required-search-param rule on its own.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Fuzzy filter across categories+regions+entities (AND between values). Good when exact tag spelling is unknown.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Exact topic filter (OR). Case sensitive — use listCategories first.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Exact region filter (OR). Case sensitive — use listRegions first.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Exact entity filter (OR). Case sensitive — use listEntities first.

parameters.name: sources
parameters.description: Publisher source ID filter (OR). Resolve names via getPublishers.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Only articles published or updated on/after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: full_content
parameters.description: Include full article body. Default false (summary only).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Skip N results for pagination. Default 0.

200.description: Articles with publisher info, engagement metrics, and trend_score

204.description: No matching articles (empty result, not an error)

400.description: Missing required search param or invalid input

get.description:
For each seed article URL, returns cross-outlet republication (`coverage`) and social/forum mentions (`mentions`).
**Input**: pass up to 128 article URLs as comma-separated query param `urls`.
**When to use**: after searchArticles — measure whether a story was picked up elsewhere or discussed on social platforms.
**Returns**: one PropagationResult per input URL (always HTTP 200; empty arrays when no propagation found).
**Related tools**: searchArticles, getTrendingArticles.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description: Seed article URLs to analyze (CSV, 1–128 valid HTTP(S) URLs)

200.description: One result object per input URL with coverage and mentions arrays

400.description: Missing urls, too many urls (>128), or invalid URL format

post.description:
Same as getArticlePropagation but accepts a JSON body — preferred when URLs contain characters awkward in query strings.
**Input**: JSON body `{ "urls": ["https://...", ...] }` with 1–128 valid HTTP(S) URLs.
**When to use**: batch propagation lookup from agent workflows that already hold URL lists in JSON.

200.description: One result object per input URL with coverage and mentions arrays

400.description: Missing urls, too many urls (>128), or invalid URL format

get.description:
Look up display metadata for one or more publisher source IDs found on article `source` fields.
Returns site name, base URL, description, and favicon for each requested source ID.
**When to use**: after searchArticles or feed endpoints to humanize source IDs in UI or agent responses.
**Required**: at least one value in `sources` (comma-separated source IDs, e.g. techcrunch.com).
**Related tools**: searchArticles, getLatestArticles.

parameters.name: sources
parameters.description: Publisher source IDs to resolve (CSV). Example: techcrunch.com,theverge.com

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default 16, max 128.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Default 0.

200.description: Publisher metadata objects keyed by source ID

204.description: No matching publishers (empty result, not an error)

post.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoint for the Beans service. Exposes Beans tools to MCP-compatible clients such as AI agents and LLM-powered applications.
